title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Russian attacks kill 2 and wound 12 as Ukrainian forces target oil tankers
stories.summary:
Russian attacks kill 2 and wound 12 as Ukrainian forces target oil tankers. Russia launched missile and drone attacks on Ukraine, killing two people and wounding 12, officials said Saturday, as Ukrainian forces damaged more than two dozens Russian tankers and other vessels in the Sea of Azov.

Al Jazeera: Russian missile and drone barrage kills six and wounds dozens in Ukraine.

Al Jazeera reports that in the latest salvo in one of the war's deadliest months, Russia hits Kyiv with ballistic missiles and 121 drones.

links.headline: Russian missile and drone barrage kills six and wounds dozens in Ukraine
links.excerpt: In the latest salvo in one of the war's deadliest months, Russia hits Kyiv with ballistic missiles and 121 drones.

links.excerpt: At least 10 people, including a child, were injured after Russia launched an overnight missile and drone attack on Kyiv early Saturday, according to Ukraine´s State Emergency Service. stories.title: Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12
stories.summary:
Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12. Hundreds of firefighters backed by helicopters and fixed-wing aircraft struggled Saturday to contain one of Spain's deadliest wildfires that killed a least 12 people.

BBC: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed.

BBC reports that at least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe Murder Investigation: Manhunt Continues After Police Release First Suspect
stories.summary:
Ann Widdecombe Murder Investigation: Manhunt Continues After Police Release First Suspect. Manhunt back on after police released man they previously thought to be suspect over the alleged murder of Reform spokesman Ann Widdecombe.

The post Ann Widdecombe Murder Investigation: Manhunt Continues After Police Release First Suspect appeared first on Breitbart .

Al Jazeera: UK police free suspect in ex-MP Ann Widdecombe murder investigation. Al Jazeera reports that the former government minister is believed to have been attacked a full day before her body was discovered.

stories.title: Typhoon Bavi makes landfall in eastern China as more than 1 million are evacuated
stories.summary:
Typhoon Bavi makes landfall in eastern China as more than 1 million are evacuated. Typhoon Bavi made landfall in China's eastern province of Zhejiang late on Saturday night and was expected to gradually weaken, state media reported.

BBC: China's second typhoon in a week makes landfall.

BBC reports that nearly two million people are evacuated from eastern Zhejiang province, with the city of Wenzhou close to the path of the storm.

stories.summary:

BBC: US Democrat Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers.

BBC reports that khanna, 49, was visiting the occupied West Bank when he was held for 90 minutes, he says.

stories.title: Mamdani’s Tribute To New York Immigrants Has A Few Exceptions
stories.summary:
Mamdani’s Tribute To New York Immigrants Has A Few Exceptions. New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani highlighted thirty immigrant neighborhoods in a recent post — and caught immediate backlash because he failed to mention Little Italy.

The famous Italian neighborhood in Lower Manhattan has been a cultural landmark since the 1880s — meaning that it was established long before many of the other neighborhoods Mamdani ... Breitbart: Italian-Americans Blast Mamdani After Excluded from Mayor's Map of NYC Immigrant Neighborhoods.

Breitbart reports that italian Americans — whose ancestors not only had a hand in building New York City, but laid the physical foundation of it — are blasting Democratic Socialist Mayor Zohran Mamdani after his administration published a map of NYC immigrant neighborhoods that excluded Little Italy.

stories.title: Classical pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against MSO over Gaza comments – video
stories.summary:
Classical pianist Jayson Gillham loses discrimination case against MSO over Gaza comments – video. A classical pianist who sued the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra has lost his case after he alleged he was unlawfully discriminated against because of his views on Israeli forces killing Palestinian journalists.

Gillham had claimed the MSO’s cancellation of a Melbourne concert he was contracted to perform on 15 August 2024 was an attempt to silence him over his stance on Israel’s attacks in Gaza. Justice Graeme Hill handed judgment down on Friday, finding that the MSO would have taken the same a.

stories.title: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed
stories.summary: A new app, HyperTexting, turns the open web into a scrollable social media-like feed. HyperTexting's new app aims to make the open web feel more like social media by turning websites, blogs, newsletters, and podcasts into a scrollable feed, while also making it easier to post to your own website.

stories.title: Reeves to launch City ‘skills compact’ committing firms to retrain staff in AI
stories.summary:
Reeves to launch City ‘skills compact’ committing firms to retrain staff in AI.

Exclusive: Plan to improve skills of thousands of financial sector workers to keep pace with tech revolution Chancellor Rachel Reeves is to announce a new City “skills compact” that will commit firms such as Barclays and Lloyds to retraining thousands of financial sector workers for the AI revolution.

The financial services skills compact will be launched on Tuesday, during what is likely to be Reeves’s final Mansion House speech to City bosses before Andy Burnham’s expected takeo.

stories.summary:
CBCP issues pastoral guide on mental health, urges compassion.

stories.title: Over 200 residents evacuate as floods hit Zamboanga del Sur town
stories.summary:
Over 200 residents evacuate as floods hit Zamboanga del Sur town. PAGADIAN CITY, Zamboanga del Sur – Continuous heavy rains brought by the enhanced southwest monsoon or habagat have triggered flooding in several barangays of Dinas town, Zamboanga del Sur, affecting homes, fishponds, and prompting pre-emptive evacuations, local officials reported on Saturday, July 11.

stories.title: Habagat prompts boat trip suspensions in Oriental Mindoro
stories.summary:
Habagat prompts boat trip suspensions in Oriental Mindoro. CALAPAN CITY, Oriental Mindoro – Strong southwest monsoon or habagat conditions enhanced by Typhoon Inday, have forced the suspension of several sea trips in Oriental Mindoro, the Philippine Ports Authority (PPA) Mimaropa said on Saturday.

In its advisory, PPA-Mimaropa said all fastcraft vessels plying the Calapan-Batangas route were cancelled due to rough seas. Also suspended.

stories.title: Diokno, De Lima say impeach trial also challenges culture of violence
stories.summary:
Diokno, De Lima say impeach trial also challenges culture of violence. MANILA, Philippines – House prosecutors and Akbayan Party-list Rep. Chel Diokno and Mamamayang Liberal Party-list Rep. Leila de Lima said Saturday that Vice President Sara Duterte’s impeachment trial goes beyond holding her accountable for alleged offenses.

It is also about rejecting a culture of violence and impunity that has become normalized under the Dutertes’ leadership. Philippine Daily Inquirer: De Lima says prosecution’s 2nd witness to bolster case vs Sara Duterte.

Philippine Daily Inquirer reports that mANILA, Philippines – As the impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte wrapped up its first week, Mamamayang Liberal Party-list Rep. Leila de Lima on Saturday said the next witness of the prosecution will strengthen the testimonies and evidence on the alleged grave threats made by Duterte.
